mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals robust labor demand, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This trend is persistent and always active.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 60% of instances when PAYEMS rises month-over-month over a rolling 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate rises above 4.5%, it indicates an aggressively restrictive monetary policy stance that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment (UNRATE) and decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in economic activity that depresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and INDPRO falls over a 3-month period
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return under the joint condition is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) compared to the overall market return.

mechanism_narrative: Within a tight labor market environment (unemployment below 4%), rising real retail sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand that drives corporate earnings and stock prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) when RSAFS rises month-over-month and UNRATE is below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5%, it indicates significant labor market slack that reduces consumer spending power and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return in periods with UNRATE above 5% is not significantly negative (p>0.05) compared to periods below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising retail sales (RSAFS) and declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an accommodating monetary policy supporting consumer demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases month-over-month and FEDFUNDS decreases over the same month
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return under the joint condition is not significantly positive (p>0.05) compared to unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent increases in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) signal rising inflation that reduces real earnings and prompts tighter monetary policy, exerting downward pressure on S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is not significantly negative in periods when CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPI below 310), rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals sustainable real economic expansion that boosts corporate profits and equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is not significantly positive when INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL is below 310 over any 3-year period.
